articles.title: HOW POSTMAN AND AI21 SHIP AI INTEGRATIONS 10X FASTER
articles.summary: Great AI products don't work in isolation - they operate across a user's entire workflow.

articles.title: GET A FREE MVP OF YOUR NEXT PRODUCT INTEGRATION ->
YOUR USERS AREN'T HUMAN ANYMORE; START BUILDING FOR AGENTS TODAY
articles.summary: Software is shifting from human operators to AI agents, making Agent Experience more important than traditional User Experience. Metrics, pricing, and product design must move from seat-based human logins to programmatic, agent-driven integration.

articles.title: HOW TO FIND YOUR “ONE METRIC TO RULE THEM ALL”
articles.summary: Early-stage startups should choose one defining metric that proves the business is working and focus the entire company on growing it. Relentless discipline around that number prevents distraction and increases the odds of reaching product-market fit.

articles.title: WHAT DO ENGINEERS MEAN WHEN WE SAY "TASTE"?
articles.summary: Engineering taste is the ability to calibrate product, system, and quality decisions based on a deep understanding of what is possible. As AI automates coding, this judgment becomes the key differentiator of great engineers, even though it is rarely taught or formalized.

articles.title: FRANCHISE THINKING AS A BUILDER
articles.summary: Product builders should think like franchisors by building systems that enable others to deliver value consistently. Real scale comes from clear processes, focused pilots, and disciplined growth rather than individual effort.

articles.title: DON'T LOSE HALF OF YOUR CUSTOMERS TO CHURN
articles.summary: Even a low monthly churn rate of 5% means losing 46% of customers annually. But, not all is lost. Churnkey helps companies like Superhuman, Gamma, and Veed reduce both voluntary and involuntary churn. Take action with the free Churn Metrics [12] product. Also read our Stripe benchmarks report [13] for tactical advice.

articles.title: MOATS
articles.summary: As AI makes software easier to clone, durable advantages will come from non-code moats such as scale, proprietary data, and trust. Long-term value depends on what you uniquely know and who relies on you, not just how fast you ship.

articles.title: CLAUDE CODE + ANALYTICS = VIBE PMING (53 MINUTE VIDEO)
articles.summary: Vibe PMing connects AI agents to analytics and workflow tools so PMs can describe problems while agents analyze data, draft specs, and route work. This shifts the PM role from manual coordination to higher-level thinking and decision-making.

articles.title: YOUR SYSTEM IS FINE. YOUR USERS AREN'T
articles.summary: Technical SLOs show whether systems are running, but business SLOs show whether users are getting value. Teams should define and measure outcome-based metrics that reflect real user success.

articles.title: YOUR PRODUCT IS NO LONGER THE CENTER OF GRAVITY
articles.summary: AI-native B2B companies are shifting from standardized products to systems tailored to each customer's workflow. Scale now comes from quickly modeling and encoding real business structures instead of imposing shared abstractions.

articles.title: BECOME AN AI-NATIVE PRODUCT MANAGER
articles.summary: AI is changing what it means to be a PM. Develop practical skills to embed AI in every workflow with expert-led GENERAL ASSEMBLY courses.

articles.title: Get four for the price of two
GREAT PMS ELIMINATE FEATURES
articles.summary: Great product managers eliminate features that do not solve real problems.

articles.title: TALKING UP TO THE USER
articles.summary: Product teams often treat users as metrics instead of capable people.
